# Quartic Lab — SEO/AEO Research Dossier (Source of Truth, June 2026)

This is the grounding document for Quartic Lab's automated SEO brain. Every recommendation is tied to a real file, route, or Firestore collection on `https://www.quarticlab.com` (Next.js 13 Pages Router, JS, Firestore-backed SSR). It separates *proven* levers from *vendor hype*, and resolves every risky claim against adversarial verdicts.

Verified repo ground truth (read at authoring time):
- `src/pages/sitemap.xml.js` — 16 hardcoded `STATIC_ROUTES`; comment says `/blog` is "intentionally excluded — see audit B1 and B2"; same `today` lastmod stamped on every URL; emits ignored `<changefreq>`/`<priority>`.
- `src/pages/robots.txt.js` — `User-agent: *` Allow, `Disallow: /api/`, AI allowlist (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, OAI-SearchBot, Google-Extended, +11 more) all `Allow: /`. This is correct — leave it.
- `src/pages/_app.js` — global Organization JSON-LD (lines 53-84) with Lahore `PostalAddress`, `contactPoint`, `sameAs` = LinkedIn + Twitter ONLY. Home-only `BreadcrumbList` with a single "Home" ListItem (lines 110-126). No LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService, no `areaServed`, no Clutch/Crunchbase/GitHub in `sameAs`.
- `src/pages/services/[slug].jsx` — routes through `Seo.jsx`, already emits `FAQPage` + `Service` schema with `areaServed` Country nodes (US/UK/PK). No BreadcrumbList.
- `src/pages/blog/[slug].jsx` — raw `<Head>` (line 479), HAS canonical + og:image (`post.heroImage`) + Article JSON-LD, but `author` is `{"@type":"Organization"}` (lines 508-512, default `post.author || "Quartic Lab"` at line 466), no `Person`, no BreadcrumbList. Displays `wordCount` as a byline metric (line 472).
- `src/pages/blog/index.js` — raw `<Head>` (line 225), self-canonical to `/blog` (line 236), NO og:image, NO CollectionPage/Breadcrumb schema, `noindex` until posts exist (line 235).
- `src/Components/CommonComponents/Seo/Seo.jsx` — centralized head; supports `canonical`, `ogImage`, twitter, `children` (for JSON-LD). Blog pages bypass it.

---

## 1. Executive summary — the 10 highest-leverage moves, ranked

1. **Get `/blog` + every published post into `sitemap.xml.js`** with a *real* per-URL `<lastmod>` from each post's `updatedAt`/`publishedDate` — and *also* fix internal linking (homepage links only to `/blog`, posts have zero cross-links). On THIS site the "weak internal linking" precondition is confirmed met, so impact is genuinely high, not just sub-optimal. *(fixing, low effort)*
2. **Route blog pages through `Seo.jsx`** and add `Person` author schema — blog `[slug].jsx` and `index.js` bypass it; the index has no og:image; the post's Article author is hardcoded `Organization`. Named human authors are one of the few repeatedly-corroborated AEO levers. *(fixing/schema-aeo, medium)*
3. **Stop chasing word count entirely** — `blog/[slug].jsx` literally renders a "X WORDS" byline (line 472). Word count is *confirmed not a ranking factor*; replace any "increase word count" task type with "cover missing subtopics/entities/intents." *(improve-content, low)*
4. **Build the thin backlink/entity profile off-site** — only a Clutch badge today. Earn brand mentions on niche agency directories + "best AI/agentic dev companies" listicles; brand mentions correlate with AI citations ~3x more than backlinks (Ahrefs, 0.664 vs 0.218). This is the single biggest AEO gap. *(backlink, high)*
5. **Rewrite blog + `service_details` openings answer-first** — each H2 phrased as the buyer's real question, with a 2-4 sentence self-contained answer first. AI engines cite *chunks*, and prose/authorship beats schema/FAQ density (Ahrefs + Google + DigitalApplied agree on direction). *(improve-content, medium)*
6. **Add `Person` author entities to Firestore `blogs` docs** (name, jobTitle, worksFor, `sameAs` LinkedIn) and render a visible byline + bio. E-E-A-T trust + AI-citation eligibility — frame as qualitative, never with vendor "+X% ranking" figures. *(schema-aeo, medium)*
7. **Publish 1-2 original-research/data posts/year** mined from real Quartic Lab delivery data (AI-MVP cost benchmark — the `ai-mvp-cost-2026` draft already exists in repo outputs). Original data is the #1 surviving link tactic and a citation magnet. *(add-blog, high)*
8. **Add comparison + cost pages** (React vs Next.js, in-house vs agency, "cost to build an AI MVP") as new `blogs` docs — listicle/comparison formats are cited ~2-2.5x more by AI engines than narrative blogs. *(add-page, medium)*
9. **Extend `BreadcrumbList` site-wide** (currently Home-only in `_app.js`) and add `LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService` + `areaServed` to the global Organization graph. Treat schema as table-stakes infrastructure, NOT a citation lever. *(schema-aeo/local, low)*
10. **Stand up free measurement** — GSC Search Analytics API → Sheet, BigQuery bulk export (turn on NOW, it's not retroactive), Bing Webmaster Tools (Copilot/ChatGPT lean on Bing), and a 20-30 prompt AEO visibility panel. Track *visibility rate*, not AI "rank." *(measurement, medium)*

**Do NOT do:** add an `llms.txt` file (dead lever), add self-serving `aggregateRating` to homepage/Organization schema (no stars since 2019, manual-action risk), strip FAQPage schema (still valid, aids Bing/LLM understanding), chase Reddit/Wikipedia for bottom-funnel B2B, or build thin `[service] in [city]` doorway pages.

---

## 2. What changed in 2025-2026 (dated)

- **Dec 11-29, 2025 — December core update.** *Google-confirmed* (Search Status Dashboard, 18-day rollout) and corroborated by 12+ trackers (Semrush, MozCast, Sistrix, AWR…). The "~15% of top-10 pages dropped from top-100" stat is *single-vendor (SE Ranking)* — attribute it, don't present as consensus. Helpful-content folded into core; authorship/Experience rewarded.
- **Feb 1, 2026 (claimed) — "Authors documentation."** *Unverified.* A blog-contributor index exists at `developers.google.com/search/blog/authors`, but Google's docs changelog shows NO Jan/Feb 2026 authorship-guidance entry. Ground author work in the durable "Creating Helpful, Reliable, People-First Content" doc, not this narrative.
- **Mar 27 - Apr 8, 2026 — March core + spam update.** Google's stated rationale was neutral. The "authority/first-party wins, aggregators lose" thesis is a *third-party read* (Amsive/Lily Ray, SISTRIX), NOT Google gospel — and it was *NOT reversed*; the May 2026 core update *reinforced* it. Spam side: SpamBrain now silently *devalues* (not penalizes) PBNs, topically-irrelevant guest posts, niche edits; topical relevance now beats raw DA. Scaled/programmatic thin pages were decimated.
- **May 7, 2026 — FAQ rich results fully retired** (confirmed *verbatim* in Google's FAQPage docs deprecation notice). FAQ search-appearance + report + Rich Results Test support drop **June 2026**; Search Console API support ends **August 2026**. Markup stays valid and is still used to understand pages + by Bing/Perplexity/LLMs — **keep it, don't optimize new FAQ markup for snippets.**
- **May 13, 2026 — GA4 native "AI Assistant" channel** (medium=`ai-assistant`) — recognizes ChatGPT/Gemini/Claude ONLY; Perplexity/Copilot still need a custom regex.
- **May 21 - Jun 2, 2026 — May core update** — reinforced March's direction; selective recovery for category-defining destinations only.
- **2026 — GSC folds AI Overviews/AI Mode into the Performance report** — a CTR dip can now be AI-Overview cannibalization, not a ranking loss. Segment before reacting. Discover became a separate stream (Feb 2026). AI-config (plain-English report setup) shipped Dec 2025.
- **2026 — Bing Webmaster Tools added an AI Performance Report** (first to separate AI citations) + Site Explorer. Newly relevant because ChatGPT/Copilot draw on Bing's index.
- **Ongoing — INP is the stable, most-failed CWV** (~43% of sites fail; ≤200ms p75). LCP ≤2.5s, CLS ≤0.1. Most AI crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot) execute **no JavaScript** — SSR is mandatory for AI visibility (Quartic Lab already SSRs key pages — good).
- **Schema ≠ AI citations (settled enough to act on).** Ahrefs difference-in-differences (1,885 pages, Aug 2025-Mar 2026): adding JSON-LD gave −4.6% AI Overviews, +2.4% AI Mode, +2.2% ChatGPT — i.e. noise. OtterlyAI's controlled test: an answer placed ONLY in FAQ schema was used by NO engine. Schema is infrastructure for rich results/entity recognition, not a citation lever.
- **`llms.txt` is dead as an AEO lever.** GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot/OAI-SearchBot/Google-Extended don't fetch it (server-log tests, 20,000+ domains); Mueller compared it to the keywords meta tag; GO MO's 6-month test showed flat AI visibility. **Do not build one.**

---

## 3. By task category

### `fixing`
- **Add `/blog` + published posts to the sitemap.** *Why:* sitemaps surface freshly-updated indexable URLs; `lastmod` is the ONE per-URL signal Google still uses; on a thin-backlink site Google leans on the sitemap. *Effort low / Impact high (conditional-high confirmed: weak internal linking is real on this site).* *How:* In `src/pages/sitemap.xml.js`, reverse the "intentionally excluded" comment. In a new `getServerSideProps` data step, query the SAME Firestore `blogs` collection used by `blog/index.js` (`status === "published"`), emit one `<url>` per `${SITE_URL}/blog/${slug}` plus `/blog`, with `<lastmod>` = `post.updatedAt || post.publishedDate`. **Replace the shared `today` lastmod on every static route with each route's true last-modified** (or omit lastmod where unknown rather than faking it).
- **Drop `<changefreq>`/`<priority>` from the sitemap.** *Why:* Google ignores both; faking the same `today` lastmod everywhere makes lastmod distrusted. *Effort low / Impact low.* *How:* In `buildSitemap()`, remove those two lines from the `<url>` template.
- **Fix internal linking to posts.** *Why:* JS-free AI crawlers depend on SSR `<a href>`; homepage links only to `/blog`, posts have zero cross-links — posts are reachable by one path that isn't even in the sitemap. *Effort low / Impact high.* *How:* Add an SSR-rendered "Insights"/related-posts block to `src/pages/index.js`, `services/[slug].jsx`, and `blog/[slug].jsx` (related by `tags`/`category`), all output in `getServerSideProps`.
- **Route blog pages through `Seo.jsx`.** *Why:* index has no og:image; both bypass centralized canonical/OG/Twitter handling → metadata drift. *Effort medium / Impact medium-high.* *How:* Replace raw `<Head>` in `blog/index.js` (line 225) and `blog/[slug].jsx` (line 479) with `<Seo>`, passing `ogImage={post.heroImage}` on posts and a default og:image on the index; keep JSON-LD as `Seo` children.
- **Keep FAQPage schema; don't optimize new FAQ markup.** *Why:* retired for Google snippets (May 7 2026) but still valid + used by Bing/LLMs. *Effort low.* *How:* Leave existing FAQPage in `index.js`, `about.js`, `contact/index.js`, `services/[slug].jsx`. Audit that JSON-LD still matches visible Q&A; remove any FAQ block that exists ONLY for schema with no visible value.

### `add-blog`
- **Original-research/data assets (1-2/year).** *Why:* original data is the #1 surviving link tactic (48.6% of SEOs) and the format AI engines preferentially cite. *Effort high / Impact high.* *How:* New `blogs` docs (`status:"published"`) with proprietary numbers from Quartic Lab projects, a visible methodology section, stats ~every 150-200 words. Start with the existing `ai-mvp-cost-2026` draft.
- **Pillar+cluster posts under each service.** *Why:* topic clusters are the 2026 mechanism for topical authority + AI-Overview visibility; a small site builds authority through depth + interlinking, not just links. *Effort high / Impact high.* *How:* Each `services/[slug]` page is a pillar; create 8-15 cluster `blogs` docs per offering with descriptive (not "learn more") internal anchors UP to the pillar and SIDEWAYS to siblings. Store cluster relationships as a field on the blog doc. Keep clean boundaries (don't cross-link AI ↔ blockchain).
- **Case studies from `projects`.** *Why:* first-hand Experience (the "E" added Dec 2022) is the AI-immune differentiator; quantified outcomes are highly citable. *Effort medium / Impact medium-high.* *How:* Expand `projects` docs into narrative case studies (problem → stack/tradeoffs → measurable outcome) and cross-link from the matching `service_details`.

### `add-page`
- **One transactional service pillar per offering.** *Why:* "X development company / hire X developers" are the highest-commercial-intent queries; intent-mismatch is the top reason sound pages fail. *Effort medium / Impact high.* *How:* Ensure one rich `services`+`service_details` record per offering: `/services/ai-ml-development`, `/services/genai-automation`, `/services/web-development`, `/services/mobile-development`, `/services/devops`, `/services/blockchain-development`, `/services/iot-development`, `/services/ui-ux-design` (these slugs already exist in `sitemap.xml.js`). Head term + company/agency/hire modifier in H1/title via `Seo.jsx`; keep `Service` JSON-LD; add a pricing-range signal + CTA above the fold.
- **Comparison pages.** *Why:* highest-converting B2B format, cited ~2-2.5x more by AI engines. *Effort medium / Impact high.* *How:* New `blogs` docs (or a `/compare/[slug]` route) for React vs Next.js, Flutter vs React Native, RAG vs fine-tuning, build vs buy, in-house vs agency, nearshore vs offshore. Lead with a pros/cons `<table>` + a "when to choose which" verdict in the first 100 words. **Must** go through `Seo.jsx` and into the sitemap or they're uncitable.
- **Cost pages.** *Why:* explicit buying-readiness; numeric answers are citation magnets. *Effort medium / Impact high.* *How:* `/blog/cost-to-build-an-ai-mvp`, `/blog/cost-to-build-a-mobile-app`, `/blog/saas-development-cost` — TL;DR price range in the first paragraph, real HTML `<table>` tiers in `contentHtml`, Article schema, sitemap + og:image.
- **Self-published "best X companies 2026" listicles** (include genuine peers) — `blogs` docs with a comparison table, refreshed quarterly. *Caveat:* transferability to a service AGENCY (vs SaaS product) is the least-proven part — treat as a bet, measure citation share before scaling.

### `improve-content`
- **Replace "increase word count" with "cover missing subtopics/entities/intents."** *Why:* Surfer's 1M-page study (Aug 2025) — text-length correlation "dropped to zero" once topical coverage was held constant; Mueller confirms length isn't a factor. *Effort low / Impact high.* *How:* When auditing a post, score it on coverage of the questions a CTO asks (for "agentic AI": orchestration, eval, cost, security, human-in-the-loop), NOT the `wordCount` already computed in `blog/[slug].jsx`. Remove/repurpose the "X WORDS" byline (line 472) so the system never optimizes for it.
- **Answer-first rewrites.** *Why:* AI engines extract chunks; the opening sentence dominates the embedding. *Effort medium / Impact high.* *How:* Editorial standard for `blogs.contentHtml` and `service_details`: H2 = the user's literal question, followed by a bolded 1-2 sentence self-contained answer, then detail. No code change.
- **Tables + tight lists for structured topics.** *Why:* among the highest-extraction LLM formats. *Effort medium / Impact medium.* *How:* Real semantic `<table>`/`<ul>`/`<ol>` in `contentHtml` (never images) for tech-stack tradeoffs, engagement models, pricing tiers.
- **Inject first-hand Experience prose.** "In our experience building X for client Y…" passages into `contentHtml` — the human signal raters/AI reward.
- **Substantive refresh cadence.** *Why:* substantive refreshes lift traffic (HubSpot case study ~106%; First Page Sage est. ~4.6 positions — both third-party estimates, cite with attribution, never as Google fact); date-only bumps do nothing. *Effort medium / Impact high.* *How:* Add `updatedAt`/`lastUpdated` to `blogs`; surface a visible "Last updated" line; feed it to Article `dateModified` (already wired at line 513) and to the new sitemap `<lastmod>`. Quarterly: substantively update 3-5 declining posts (refresh code samples to current Next.js/React, add entities).

### `technical`
- **Keep the robots.txt AI allowlist as-is** (`robots.txt.js` is correct) and verify in CDN/hosting logs which bots actually fetch which pages. If OAI-SearchBot never hits `/blog/`, that corroborates the sitemap-exclusion gap. **Do NOT add `llms.txt`.**
- **Confirm SSR returns full `contentHtml`** in the initial response for blog + service pages (it does via `getServerSideProps`) — prose-first, JS-free rendering is what JS-less AI crawlers extract.
- **INP** (most-failed CWV): load GTM/GA/Clutch widget via `next/script strategy="lazyOnload"` (or Pages-Router `worker`/Partytown). The `useEnhanceArticle` client hook in `blog/[slug].jsx` (~line 106) manipulates article HTML — ensure it doesn't run one long synchronous task on large `contentHtml`; chunk with `requestIdleCallback`/`scheduler.yield()`. *Treat any "60-65%"/"27ms" figures as observational from other sites — gate stakeholder targets on Quartic Lab's own field data.*
- **LCP/CLS:** add `priority`+`fetchpriority="high"` to the home + service-detail hero `next/image`; serve AVIF/WebP; pass explicit width/height to the Firestore `post.heroImage` `next/image` in `blog/[slug].jsx`; reserve a fixed min-height for the lazy Clutch widget. Self-hosted fonts already mitigate font CLS — keep `font-display: swap`.
- **Self-referencing canonicals everywhere** (already via `Seo.jsx`/blog heads). `blog/index.js` hardcodes canonical to `/blog` (line 236) — fine while the listing is a single SSR page; if pagination is ever added, make the canonical reflect the paginated URL.
- **Crawl budget is a non-issue** (<1,000 URLs) — spend effort on internal links + sitemap freshness instead. Keep `Disallow: /api/`.

### `schema-aeo`
- **Person author schema on posts.** *Why:* named authors are one of few corroborated AEO/E-E-A-T levers. *Effort medium / Impact medium.* *How:* In `blog/[slug].jsx` Article JSON-LD (lines 508-512), replace `author:{"@type":"Organization"}` with `author:{"@type":"Person",name,url,sameAs:[LinkedIn,...],jobTitle,worksFor:{"@id":SITE_URL+"/#organization"}}` sourced from a new `author` object on `blogs` docs; render a visible byline + bio. Avoid fake experts.
- **`BreadcrumbList` site-wide.** *Why:* still a supported 2026 rich-result type; cheap structural/entity signal. *Effort medium / Impact medium.* *How:* Generalize the Home-only helper in `_app.js` (lines 110-126) into a route-keyed component applied on `/services`, `/services/[slug]` (Home › Services › {name}), `/blog`, `/blog/[slug]` (Home › Blog › Post), projects. Pull labels from the docs already loaded in each `getServerSideProps`.
- **CollectionPage + og:image on `/blog`.** *How:* In `blog/index.js`, via `Seo.jsx`, add a default og:image and a `CollectionPage` JSON-LD listing recent posts + Home › Blog breadcrumb.
- **Treat all schema as infrastructure, not a citation lever.** Ahrefs/OtterlyAI: adding JSON-LD produced no citation uplift; engines extract visible HTML and ignore hidden schema at answer time. Keep schema for rich results/entity recognition only.

### `backlink`
*(Highest-leverage off-site category — see Section 5 for the ranked weekly plan.)*
- Earn brand mentions on niche category directories + "best AI/agentic/Next.js dev companies" listicles (brand mentions ~3x backlinks for AI citation — Ahrefs 0.664 vs 0.218, *correlation not causation*).
- Claim/deepen Clutch, GoodFirms, DesignRush, TheManifest, G2, Crunchbase, LinkedIn Company Page — NAP byte-identical to `_app.js` Organization schema.
- Earn ecosystem/partner links (cloud/DevOps/AI-tooling partner pages) — safest dofollow links for an agency.
- Build the entity: enrich Organization `sameAs` (add Clutch/Crunchbase/GitHub — currently only LinkedIn + Twitter), consistent brand facts, founder/engineer bylined content.
- Branded-dominant anchor profile (35-50% branded); avoid commercial exact-match anchors from paid/guest placements.
- AVOID PBNs, bulk paid links, mass directory spam, scaled guest posts — devalued (zeroed equity), not just penalized.

### `local`
- **Add `LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService` to the global graph.** *Why:* closes a known gap; supports entity grounding. *Effort low / Impact medium.* *How:* In `_app.js`, extend Organization to `@type:["Organization","ProfessionalService"]` in one `@graph`, keep the existing Lahore `PostalAddress`, and add `areaServed` as Country nodes (US, GB, DE, AE, SA…). **Omit `aggregateRating`** (no self-serving stars).
- **One legitimate Google Business Profile** for the Lahore office: primary category "Software Company," service areas = Lahore + nearby cities ONLY (June 24 2025 ban on country/state service areas; ~2h cap). Value is brand/Pakistan-MENA visibility + knowledge-panel trust, NOT US ranking (proximity is non-hackable). Add the GBP Maps URL to `sameAs`.
- **NAP single source of truth.** Extract the address/phone (already in `Footer.jsx`/`contact`) to a shared constant reused in `_app.js` PostalAddress; audit Clutch/LinkedIn to match byte-for-byte.
- **No hreflang** (single English language) and **no thin `[service] in [city]` doorway pages.** If you build market pages later, make ≤3 substantively-unique high-intent pages (`/locations/[slug]` via a new `locations` collection), routed through `Seo.jsx` and added to the sitemap — *and treat on-page-only country targeting for a PK-hosted .com as an unproven bet to measure in GSC by country, not assume.*

### `measurement`
- **GSC Search Analytics API → Google Sheet** (Apps Script, 50,000 rows/day, pull offset 3 days for the lag). Segment by `/blog/` prefix even while blog URLs are missing from the sitemap.
- **GSC → BigQuery bulk export — turn on NOW** (not retroactive; free within ~10 GiB storage + 2 TiB query/mo for this site size). Partition on `data_date`, query once/day into a stored table.
- **Bing Webmaster Tools** — verify the domain, submit the sitemap (after the blog fix), use the AI Performance Report as a free Copilot/ChatGPT-citation baseline; confirm robots.txt isn't blocking Bingbot (it isn't).
- **AEO visibility panel** — 20-30 buyer prompts, run 3-5x monthly across ChatGPT/Perplexity/Gemini; track *visibility rate* (% of runs the brand appears), NOT a single "rank."
- **GA4 AI-referral custom channel** (regex for chatgpt.com, perplexity.ai, gemini.google.com, copilot.microsoft.com — native channel misses Perplexity/Copilot) + **server-log AI-bot analysis**.
- **CWV field data** via `web-vitals` → GA, tagged by route (`/blog/[slug]` vs `/services/[slug]`), alert at 80% of thresholds.

---

## 4. AEO/GEO playbook — get cited in AI Overviews / ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini

**The mental model:** AEO in 2026 is mostly fundamentals (crawlable SSR HTML, topical authority, freshness, off-site brand mentions, named authors, answer-first prose) plus a few formatting moves — NOT a new discipline. AI referrals are still ~1-2% of B2B traffic, so this is a **brand-influence play, not a traffic channel** — budget accordingly.

**Proven levers (do these):**
1. **Discoverability + freshness** — blog in the sitemap with real `lastmod`; visible "Last updated" dates. (Recency helps directionally; ignore the unverified "3.2x within 30 days" multiplier as a KPI.)
2. **Off-page entity building** — ~85% of AI-feeding brand mentions come from third-party pages; brand mentions correlate ~3x more than backlinks. This is the dominant gap given the thin profile.
3. **Named human authors** (`Person` schema + visible bio) — corroborated; frame qualitatively, never with "+47%/+34%" point estimates (vendor-reported, no CIs/p-values — direction only).
4. **Answer-first chunks, tables, tight lists** in visible HTML — engines extract visible prose and ignore hidden schema.
5. **Original data + opinion density** — unique facts/stats are preferentially cited.
6. **Per-engine source strategy** — only ~11% ChatGPT/Perplexity domain overlap (corroborated by Averi + Passionfruit). Gemini tilts to brand-owned + editorial roundups; ChatGPT spreads across third-party/directories; Perplexity is Reddit/review-heavy; Google AI favors YouTube. Use platform-specific placements, not one playbook.

**Hype / do NOT do:**
- **`llms.txt`** — dead lever; no major engine fetches it; Mueller refuses to support it. Harmless but worthless. **Do not build one.**
- **Schema density for citations** — Ahrefs DiD: no uplift (−4.6% AI Overviews); OtterlyAI: schema-only answers used by no engine. Keep schema for rich results/entity recognition only.
- **Keyword-stuffed FAQs / brand-mention-density theater** — test within noise.
- **Chasing Reddit/Wikipedia for bottom-funnel B2B** — they dominate broad-keyword studies but barely register for specific agency/software queries; niche directories + industry blogs win there. (The exact engine percentages — 46.7% Reddit etc. — are volatile vendor snapshots; treat as directional rankings, not targets.)
- **"Rank #1 = cited"** — decoupled; ~90% of ChatGPT citations come from URLs ranked 21+. Do both classic SEO and explicit AEO/off-site presence.

**The contested-but-decided call:** the net role of schema for AI extraction is genuinely contested, but the *causal* designs (Ahrefs DiD, OtterlyAI controlled) outweigh correlational vendor stats (38.5% vs 32%, "2.5x") that confound schema with site quality. **Verdict: schema is table-stakes, not a lever — spend the effort on prose, authors, freshness, and off-site mentions.**

---

## 5. Backlink & digital-PR plan (ranked by ROI for an agency)

**Prerequisite (Week 0):** ship the blog-sitemap + `Seo.jsx` + internal-linking fixes so earned links/citations point to discoverable, crawlable pages that pass equity. Skipping this wastes every link you earn.

**Ranked by ROI:**
1. **Deepen Clutch + claim the directory tier** (GoodFirms, DesignRush, TheManifest, G2, Crunchbase) with complete service taxonomy matching `services`/`service_details` and phone-verified reviews. *Highest ROI:* doubles as AI-citation source + bottom-funnel lead source; verified Clutch reviews auto-feed TheManifest "best-of" lists. NAP byte-identical to `_app.js`.
2. **Quarterly original-research digital PR** from real delivery data → pitch via Featured.com (rehomed HARO) + Qwoted + direct journalist outreach. One data campaign earns DR50-85 editorial links + durable AI citations.
3. **Get included in "best AI/agentic/Next.js dev companies 2026" listicles** — use an AI-visibility tool to find which domains are cited for your target prompts, then pursue those exact lists (reputational ingestion lag ~30-90 days).
4. **Founder/lead-engineer thought leadership** — LinkedIn (both personal posts AND Company Page — ChatGPT/Google pull from member posts, Perplexity from Company Pages), plus expert-sourcing 3-5x/week via Featured/Qwoted/SOS using a *named human*. Builds the personal entity LLMs associate with the brand.
5. **Ecosystem/partner links** — cloud/DevOps/AI-tooling partner pages, alliance member pages mapped to the service taxonomy → link to the matching `service_details`/case study.
6. **Enrich Organization `sameAs`** in `_app.js` (add Clutch, Crunchbase, GitHub; currently only LinkedIn + Twitter) and convert unlinked brand mentions to links.
7. **Genuine, non-promotional Reddit/YouTube presence** for the platforms AI cites (directional) — niche dev/startup/AI subreddits, transcript-rich explainer videos repurposed from research posts.

**Anchor governance:** ~35-50% branded, 10-15% naked URL, 15-25% generic, ≤15% exact-match. Never commercial exact-match from paid/guest placements (Dec 2025 spam policy names it explicitly).

**Avoid:** PBNs, bulk paid dofollow, mass low-quality directories, scaled generalist guest posts, niche edits on thin aged domains — all devalued to zero equity, profile-polluting. Run one backlink audit; disavow only clearly toxic patterns.

---

## 6. Content-cluster map (pillar + cluster across 8 service areas)

Each `services/[slug]` page = the pillar (transactional intent). Cluster posts = `blogs` docs linking UP to the pillar + SIDEWAYS to siblings. Mix of informational/commercial intent. Keep clean boundaries between clusters.

| Pillar (service) | Example cluster posts | Target intent |
|---|---|---|
| **AI/ML** `/services/ai-ml-development` | "Cost to build an AI MVP (2026 benchmark)" · "RAG vs fine-tuning: when to choose which" · "How to evaluate an LLM in production" · "Build vs buy: ML platform" | commercial / informational |
| **GenAI & agentic** `/services/genai-automation` | "How to build a production AI agent" · "Agentic automation ROI: data from N deployments" · "Human-in-the-loop patterns for agents" · "Best agentic AI development companies 2026" | informational / commercial / listicle |
| **Web (React/Next/Node)** `/services/web-development` | "Next.js vs React: what to choose in 2026" · "Pages Router vs App Router migration cost" · "Hire Next.js developers: in-house vs agency" · "SaaS development cost breakdown" | commercial / transactional |
| **Mobile** `/services/mobile-development` | "Flutter vs React Native (2026)" · "Cost to build a mobile app" · "Native vs cross-platform: decision guide" | comparison / commercial |
| **DevOps** `/services/devops` | "CI/CD for startup teams" · "Cloud cost optimization post-mortem" · "DevOps maturity checklist for CTOs" | informational |
| **Blockchain** `/services/blockchain-development` | "Smart-contract audit checklist" · "When blockchain is the wrong choice" · "Build vs buy: web3 infra" | informational / commercial |
| **IoT** `/services/iot-development` | "IoT architecture patterns" · "Edge vs cloud processing tradeoffs" · "IoT MVP cost guide" | informational / commercial |
| **UI/UX** `/services/ui-ux-design` | "Design-system ROI case study" · "UX audit checklist for B2B SaaS" · "Figma-to-Next.js handoff" | informational |

Every cluster post: answer-first H2s, a comparison/cost `<table>`, named `Person` author, real "Last updated" date, 3-5 descriptive internal links to its pillar, in sitemap, routed through `Seo.jsx`.

---

## 7. Myths to NOT act on

| Myth | Corrected reality |
|---|---|
| **"Increase word count → ranks better"** | **VERDICT: word count is NOT a ranking factor (confirmed).** Surfer (1M pages, Aug 2025): length correlation dropped to zero once topical coverage was controlled; Mueller confirms it. **Remove "increase word count" as a task type entirely; remove the "X WORDS" byline in `blog/[slug].jsx`; replace with "cover missing subtopics/entities/intents."** Length serves completeness, never a target. |
| "AI content is penalized for being AI" | Google is method-neutral; only scaled low-value content is demoted. |
| "Publishing `llms.txt` gets you cited" | No major engine consumes it; Google refuses to support it. Don't build one. |
| "More schema/JSON-LD boosts AI citations" | Ahrefs DiD: no uplift (−4.6% AI Overviews). Schema is infrastructure for rich results, not a citation lever. |
| "FAQPage still earns SERP dropdowns" | False since May 7 2026. Keep markup (valid, aids Bing/LLMs); don't optimize new FAQ markup for snippets. |
| "`changefreq`/`priority` influence crawling" | Ignored. Only accurate `lastmod` matters. Remove them from `sitemap.xml.js`. |
| "Author bylines give +X% rankings" | No credible primary source; Mueller calls bios "a quality/UX thing, not for ranking." Do them for trust + AI-citation eligibility; never quote vendor "+34%/+48%" figures. |
| "Information gain is Google's #1 live signal" | Patent US12013887B2 is assistant-scoped + speculative for organic ranking. Pursue originality as best-practice; do NOT hardcode an "information-gain score" into the brain. |
| "A Lahore GBP can rank for US/EU local searches" | Proximity is non-hackable; June 24 2025 bans country/state service areas. GBP = brand/Pakistan-MENA only. |
| "Add our Clutch/Google stars to homepage schema" | No self-serving review stars for Organization/LocalBusiness since 2019; manual-action risk. Show ratings as a UI badge. |
| "We need hreflang for US/EU/MENA" | hreflang is for language/region variants; unnecessary for an English-only site (~75% of implementations have errors). |
| "Mass `[service] in [city]` pages win local" | Thin doorway pages are devalued/filtered. ≤3 substantively-unique market pages max. |
| "Backlinks/DA are the main lever for AI recommendation" | Brand mentions correlate ~3x more (Ahrefs 0.664 vs 0.218, correlation not causation). |
| "Mass guest posts on high-DA sites build authority fast" | Devalued March 2026; topical relevance now beats raw DA. |
| "Page-1 ranking = AI citation" | Decoupled; ~90% of ChatGPT citations are from URLs ranked 21+. |
| "March 2026's first-party shift was reversed" | NOT reversed — May 2026 reinforced it. Don't plan around a rollback. |
| "GSC CTR/impression drop = ranking loss" | Could be AI-Overview cannibalization (now folded into Performance). Segment before reacting. |
| "GA4 AI Assistant channel captures all AI referrals" | Only ChatGPT/Gemini/Claude; Perplexity/Copilot need a custom regex; crawlers live only in logs. |
| "BigQuery export recovers history past 16 months" | NOT retroactive — only forward from setup. Turn it on now. |
| "You can track AI 'rank' like Google rank" | SparkToro/Gumshoe (2,961 prompts): lists repeat <1%. Track visibility rate over many runs. |

---

## 8. Measurement & weekly cadence

**KPIs (per channel):**
- *Search:* clicks/impressions/avg-position by `/blog/`, `/services/`, `/compare/` segments; striking-distance count (pos 5-20 AND impressions ≥100); high-impression/low-CTR pages; content-decay flags.
- *AEO:* visibility rate across the 20-30-prompt panel per engine; AI-referral sessions (GA4 custom channel); AI-bot crawl hits per path (server logs).
- *Off-page:* referring domains, unlinked brand mentions, directory profile completeness, review velocity.
- *CWV:* p75 INP/LCP/CLS by route (field data).

**How to read GSC (2026 caveats):** 3-day lag; average position is impression-weighted, not live rank; AI Overviews/AI Mode are folded into Performance (segment these before treating a CTR dip as a ranking loss); UI export caps at 1,000 rows + 16-month window → use the API + BigQuery for full data.

**Striking-distance band:** the brain should use **position 5-20 AND impressions ≥100** (contested across sources as 8-20 / 11-20) — validate against Quartic Lab's actual GSC distribution before hardcoding.

**Weekly → task-list pipeline (the brain reads the GSC Sheet, not Looker):**
- **Weekly:** WoW compare. (a) *Defend* — any URL that lost ≥3 positions OR ≥20% clicks → defend task (segment out AI-Overview impressions first). (b) *Push* — pos 5-20 + impressions ≥100 → improve-content task mapped to the right `service_details`/`blogs` doc. (c) *CTR gap* — high-impression/low-CTR → title/meta rewrite. **Critical routing flag:** for static/service pages the fix is a `Seo.jsx` prop change; for blog pages the fix touches the raw `<Head>` in `blog/[slug].jsx`/`index.js` — tag each task with the code path until blog pages are migrated to `Seo.jsx`.
- **Monthly:** striking-distance progress; content decay (6mo-over-6mo, ignore pages <3 months old) → refresh tasks (update `contentHtml`, bump visible date + Article `dateModified` + sitemap `lastmod`); backlink delta; AEO visibility rate.
- **Quarterly:** full content/decay audit; competitive pass; refresh 3-5 declining posts substantively.

Decay and AEO run as *separate monthly jobs* so their noise doesn't flood the weekly list.

---

## 9. Verdicts ledger

| Claim | Verdict | What we'll do |
|---|---|---|
| Dec 2025 volatility is single-vendor (SE Ranking), not Google | **mixed** | State the update was Google-confirmed (Dec 11-29) + corroborated by 12+ trackers; attribute ONLY the "~15%" stat to SE Ranking. |
| Mar 2026 first-party thesis is third-party + partly reversed | **mixed** | Treat "first-party/authority wins" as credible third-party signal (act on it); do NOT plan around a reversal — May 2026 reinforced it. |
| Author-bio ranking-gain figures are SEO-vendor sourced | **confirmed** | Do author work for trust/AEO; never cite "+X% ranking" vendor figures. Frame qualitatively. |
| DigitalApplied +47/34/28% are vendor-reported, direction corroborated | **confirmed** | Use only to set direction/priority (authorship/prose > schema/FAQ); never hardcode as KPI targets; benchmark our own citation share. |
| Schema has zero/negative effect on AI citations (contested) | **confirmed** | Treat existing JSON-LD as infrastructure; reallocate to discoverability, prose, authors, backlinks — not more markup. |
| Freshness 3.2x / 70%→20% overlap are opaque vendor stats | **confirmed** | Run a refresh cadence + surface "Last updated"; do NOT use 3.2x or 70%→20% as KPIs or publish them as fact; measure our own citation share. |
| FAQ deprecation timeline (May 7 / June / Aug 2026) | **confirmed** | Keep FAQPage schema on all 4 page types; stop optimizing FAQ markup for snippets; redirect effort to blog sitemap + LocalBusiness + blog-index schema. |
| Blog sitemap exclusion is sub-optimal not high-priority harm | **mixed** | Verified weak internal linking on THIS site → impact is genuinely high. Fix sitemap AND add homepage→post + related-post internal links. |
| INP magnitudes (60-65% / 27ms) are observational | **confirmed** | Implement lazyOnload + scheduler.yield as directional wins; label numbers as other-site observations; gate targets on our own p75 field data. |
| Information-gain patent is a live dominant ranking signal | **confirmed (it is not dominant/confirmed)** | Pursue originality; do NOT hardcode an information-gain score/rubric into the brain's scoring logic. |
| Refresh numbers (~106% / 4.6 positions / "#6 factor") | **confirmed (third-party estimates)** | Prioritize substantive refreshes on pos 4-20 posts after fixing the sitemap; cite figures with explicit third-party attribution, never as Google fact. |
| Google added an "Authors" doc section ~Feb 2026 | **mixed** | A blog-author *index* exists; the "documentation update" framing is unverified. Ground author work in the durable People-First Content doc, not this. |
| Brand mentions r≈0.664 vs backlinks r≈0.218 (~3x) | **mixed** | Source is Ahrefs' own cross-validated study (cite May/Dec 2025 + "correlation, not causation"); use for directional priority (mentions/PR over link-count), not as predictive coefficients. |
| Per-engine citation %s (46.7% Reddit, 11% overlap, etc.) | **confirmed (directional only)** | Pursue the durably-cited platforms (Reddit/LinkedIn/G2/Clutch/YouTube) per engine; never treat a single vendor snapshot percentage as a fixed target. |

---

**Bottom line for the brain:** the ordered backlog is (1) blog→sitemap + internal links + `Seo.jsx` + `Person` authors, (2) kill word-count optimization, (3) off-site entity/mention building, (4) answer-first + original-data content in pillar/cluster shape, (5) `BreadcrumbList` site-wide + `LocalBusiness`/`areaServed` as table-stakes schema, (6) free measurement loop tracking *visibility rate* not AI "rank." Schema density and `llms.txt` are explicitly out of scope as citation levers.

Relevant files: `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/pages/sitemap.xml.js`, `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/pages/robots.txt.js`, `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/pages/_app.js`, `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/Components/CommonComponents/Seo/Seo.jsx`, `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/pages/blog/index.js`, `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/pages/blog/[slug].jsx`, `/Users/sarohy/Projects/zweidevs-website/src/pages/services/[slug].jsx`.
